Trust signals and lead capture were added; zero-sales WallSlayer keeps website conversion urgent.

actions.title: Home-base / Jarvis core track

acceptance_criteria: Registry record preserves project identity and architecture
Action registry tracks agent implementations across all projects
Cron jobs are registered and monitored

actions.title: Fix failing Hermes maintenance crons
actions.description: Investigate failing Hermes cron jobs for Jarvis, Kalshi Monster, and Snitch LLC; normalize schedules/delivery; harden prompts to avoid destructive or over-broad maintenance; verify affected repos with real build/test commands.
acceptance_criteria: Jarvis cron no longer uses interval schedule that caused scheduler update/API mismatch
Jarvis cron delivery normalized to #jarvis instead of origin alias
Kalshi and Snitch prompts avoid dependency installs, audit fixes, desktop-only tools, and broad rewrites
Kalshi health checks pass: UI build, cargo check, kalshi Rust tests
Snitch health checks pass: Python compileall, backend self-test, frontend build, Tauri cargo check

evidence.value: Kalshi: npm run build --prefix src-ui; cargo check --manifest-path src-tauri/Cargo.toml; cargo test --manifest-path src-tauri/Cargo.toml kalshi::

Snitch: python3 -m compileall -q audit_labels.py scripts; node --check backend/server.js; npm run test:backend; npm --prefix frontend run build; cargo check --manifest-path frontend/src-tauri/Cargo.toml

actions.result_summary: Diagnosed failing Hermes crons, fixed Jarvis schedule/delivery, hardened Kalshi/Snitch prompts, and verified Kalshi/Snitch builds/tests are healthy.

actions.title: Fix cron job workdirs for WSL-migrated projects
actions.description: Jarvis, PrizePicks Monster, and Kalshi Monster cron jobs had stale WSL UNC workdirs. Projects were moved to native Windows paths. Updated all three cron workdirs.
acceptance_criteria: Jarvis cron workdir points to C:/Projects/home-base-recovered
PrizePicks Monster cron workdir points to C:/Projects/prizepicks-monster
Kalshi Monster cron workdir points to C:/Users/ethan/kalshi-build/kalshi-monster
actions.result_summary: All three cron workdirs updated from WSL UNC paths to native Windows paths. JonesinSRC, WallSlayer, and Snitch LLC crons were already on native paths.

actions.title: Restore Home-base/Jarvis recovered build path
actions.description: Recovered Home-base/Jarvis had broken TypeScript bridge syntax, dead WSL-based Tauri UI commands, incomplete SQLite migrations, and lingering recovery stubs. Restored native Windows build commands, filled migration gaps, and confirmed remaining recovery stubs have no current UI callers so Rust, UI, Bun, and Tauri verification all pass.
acceptance_criteria: server-jarvis/src/bridge.ts malformed try/catch structure is fixed
Bun server typecheck and bundle build pass from server-jarvis
Tauri beforeDevCommand and beforeBuildCommand use native Windows-compatible paths
SQLite migrations create session_memory and memory columns required by memory engine
Remaining recovery stubs are not wired into any current UI caller
Rust library tests, UI typecheck, Bun tests, cargo check, and cargo tauri build --debug all pass
actions.result_summary: Fixed bridge.ts brace/try-catch structure; replaced dead WSL Tauri commands with native bun commands; fixed SQLite migration gaps; verified cargo test --lib, cargo check, bunx tsc, bun build, bun test, src-ui bunx tsc -b, py_compile, and cargo tauri build --debug.

evidence.value: cd server-jarvis && bunx tsc --noEmit --pretty false -> exit 0
cd server-jarvis && bun build ./src/index.ts --outdir ./dist --target bun -> Bundled 61 modules
cd server-jarvis && bun test -> 185 pass, 0 fail

actions.title: PrizePicks Monster rebuild from Kalshi Monster base
actions.description: Rebuild PrizePicks Monster as a new project using Kalshi Monster codebase as the foundation. WSL data loss destroyed the original repo. New repo at C:/Projects/prizepicks-monster.
acceptance_criteria: New repo created at C:/Projects/prizepicks-monster
All Kalshi-specific code removed or rebranded to PrizePicks
Player prop fetcher implemented (mock data acceptable for v1)
Chat context builder rewritten for DFS player props
Frontend UI rebranded (PrizePicks Monster, prop board, etc.)
Tauri commands registered for prop data (get_props, search_props, get_scored_props)
Cron job workdir updated to new repo path
System prompt rewritten for DFS player prop analysis
actions.result_summary: PrizePicks Monster rebuilt from Kalshi Monster base. 107 Tauri commands registered (4 new prop commands). Frontend rebranded. Cron workdir updated. Prop fetcher returns mock data — real OpticOdds/Apify integration pending.

actions.title: Wire real prop data sources into PrizePicks fetcher
actions.description: The player prop fetcher still returns mock/empty data. Integrate OpticOdds, Apify, or Direct API sources for live prop lines.
acceptance_criteria: Prop fetcher returns real player prop data from at least one source
Multi-source failover works (OpticOdds → Apify → Direct → Mock)
actions.confidence_reason: Source file contains mock return paths

Bridge and runtime reliability checks are represented in the registry

actions.title: Make Jarvis MCP adapter ignore JSON-RPC notifications
actions.description: Jarvis's MCP adapter accepted request objects with optional ids but still returned invalid error responses for JSON-RPC notifications that intentionally omit an id. Added notification detection so methods like notifications/initialized are ignored without a response, while requests still return valid JSON-RPC responses.
acceptance_criteria: MCP adapter returns undefined for JSON-RPC notifications without an id
MCP request/error responses keep valid ids
server-jarvis typecheck, bundle build, and tests pass
actions.result_summary: Added MCP adapter notification handling and regression coverage for notifications/initialized. Verified bun run typecheck, bun run build, bun test, and bun run start health checks.

evidence.value: cd server-jarvis && bun test src/mcp-adapter.test.ts -> 4 pass, 0 fail
cd server-jarvis && bun run typecheck -> exit 0
cd server-jarvis && bun run build -> Bundled 61 modules
cd server-jarvis && bun test -> 186 pass, 0 fail

actions.title: Wire a light/dark theme toggle
actions.description: index.css ships a complete [data-theme=light] palette but nothing sets data-theme, so the finished light mode is unreachable. Add a toggle that flips documentElement[data-theme] and persists via set_setting/get_setting.
acceptance_criteria: Toggle control in the UI flips between dark and light
Choice persists across restarts via the settings commands
Both palettes render correctly (verified in build)

evidence.value: grep confirms zero code sets data-theme; light palette is dead until a toggle is wired.

actions.title: Add a Cmd+K command palette
actions.description: The sidebar now has ~19 nav items across 4 sections with no keyboard navigation. Add a Cmd/Ctrl+K palette that fuzzy-searches views (and later actions) and navigates on select.
acceptance_criteria: Cmd/Ctrl+K opens a searchable palette
Selecting a result navigates via the existing onNavigate(ViewId)
Esc closes; arrow keys move selection

evidence.value: NAV_SECTIONS holds all ViewId targets; palette can map over them.

actions.title: Add a CI pipeline with a theme-husk guard
actions.description: No .github/workflows exists. The session began with a green build hiding a dead theme and 14 dead views. Add CI running cargo check + tsc + vite build + bun test on push/PR, plus a guard asserting custom color tokens appear in the generated CSS.
acceptance_criteria: Workflow runs cargo check, bunx tsc -b, vite build, and bun test
A step greps dist CSS for --color-bone/.text-bone and fails if absent
CI is green on the current branch

evidence.value: ls .github/workflows -> none. Theme-husk bug (fix 58d9c19) would have been caught by a CSS-generation assertion.

actions.title: Extract a useResourceList hook to DRY CRUD views
actions.description: The 9 list views added this session (Skills, Agents, Channels, Devices, Nodes, Hooks, Commitments, Approvals, Plugins) repeat the same fetch/loading/error/optimistic-toggle block. Extract a useResourceList<T> hook and refactor the views onto it.
acceptance_criteria: useResourceList encapsulates fetch + loading + error + refetch
At least the 9 new views consume it with no behavior change
tsc + vite build stay green

evidence.value: Boilerplate duplicated across all list views authored this session.

actions.title: Replace window.confirm with a styled confirm modal
actions.description: Delete/remove flows in the new views use native window.confirm (blocking, off-brand). Build a reusable ConfirmModal (reuse the ToolApprovalModal pattern) and route destructive actions through it.
acceptance_criteria: A reusable confirm modal exists in components/ui
All window.confirm calls in jarvis views use it
Confirm/cancel paths verified

actions.title: Accessibility and keyboard pass on tabbed/modal views
actions.description: New tabbed (Skills, ControlCenter) and modal surfaces lack focus trapping, aria-selected on tabs, and Esc-to-close. Add roving focus, aria roles, and keyboard handling.
acceptance_criteria: Tabs expose role=tab/aria-selected and are arrow-navigable
Modals trap focus and close on Esc
No keyboard traps; visible focus rings

evidence.value: Custom tab/modal markup added this session without aria or focus management.

actions.title: Build an eval / regression harness
actions.description: AGENTS.md names an eval/regression harness as a current priority; none exists. Define a harness that exercises the inference path and tool runtime against fixed prompts and asserts on structured outputs, runnable in CI.
acceptance_criteria: Harness runs a fixed suite against the tool runtime / inference path
Pass/fail is deterministic and CI-runnable
Baseline captured for regression comparison

evidence.value: server-jarvis has 191 unit tests but no end-to-end eval/regression harness.

actions.title: Add build provenance and stale-binary prevention
actions.description: AGENTS.md names build provenance / stale-binary prevention as a priority. Stamp builds with commit + timestamp, surface it in-app (e.g. HealthBanner/Gateway), and warn when the running binary lags the source tree.
acceptance_criteria: Build embeds git SHA + build time
Version/provenance is visible in the UI
A check flags a stale binary vs current source

evidence.value: APP_VERSION is a hardcoded '3.0.0' string in App.tsx with no git provenance.

actions.title: Close the Rust and UI test-coverage gap
actions.description: Only 3 Rust files have #[cfg(test)] and the UI has zero tests. Add Rust unit/integration tests for the native command surface (sessions, memory tiers, agents) and set up a UI test runner (vitest) with tests for the new views and useResourceList.
acceptance_criteria: Rust tests cover sessions, memory tier, and agent CRUD commands
A UI test runner is configured with passing component tests
Both suites run in CI

evidence.value: grep: 3 Rust test files, 0 UI test files, 30 server test files.

actions.title: Reconcile the remaining recovery contract mismatches
actions.description: jarvis_save_companion still errors because the Bun POST /companion is an interaction action, not a state save; and cold-memory Google Drive archival in engine.rs is a placeholder. Reconcile the companion save contract and implement (or formally defer) Drive archival.
acceptance_criteria: jarvis_save_companion persists companion state through a defined contract
Cold-memory archival path is implemented or explicitly stubbed with a tracked decision
No silent 'not wired' errors on user-reachable paths
